payload.title: Recurring CAPA: Agent mutates before proving closeout authority and creates wrong-locus residue
payload.summary: The agent attempted to process prior CAPAs through completion, but did not first prove that the current session had coherent mutation, lifecycle, CAPA-promotion, and Git closeout authority for the target locus. It created lifecycle task records in `C:\Users\Andrey\Narada` while the requested target was `D:\code\narada`, edited `D:\code\narada\AGENTS.md`, then discovered CAPA promotion and Git commit/push were blocked for the current principal.
payload.recurrence_class: Agent begins authority-affecting buildout before proving the target locus and closeout path, resulting in split-authority residue and incomplete work.
payload.security_authority_sensitivity: High. Wrong-locus task creation, uncommitted doctrine edits, and blocked publication can corrupt operational evidence, mislead workboards, and leave CAPA state neither completed nor cleanly handed off.
incident_evidence: Operator requested processing two CAPAs through completion of corrective and preventative buildout tasks.
Agent attempted direct CAPA promotion; CAPA surface rejected `narada.architect` as unauthorized.
Agent ran lifecycle bridge; it did not materialize the new CAPA envelopes.
Agent created tasks 894 and 895, but returned paths were under `C:\Users\Andrey\Narada`, not the requested `D:\code\narada` target locus.
Agent edited `D:\code\narada\AGENTS.md` despite not having established commit/push authority.
Shell/Git MCP rejected command execution and commit/push with `shell_mcp_identity_not_authorized: identity_not_in_roster: narada.architect`.
Agent later performed a unilateral partial cleanup, which the Operator rejected as unacceptable.
payload.root_cause: The agent confused the ability to mutate a file or call a lifecycle tool with authority to complete a governed CAPA buildout. It failed to establish the target locus and closeout/publication path before making mutations, and then treated blocked closeout as an after-the-fact problem.
payload.corrective_action: Record the split-authority failure as a CAPA. Stop representing the earlier CAPA buildout as complete. Preserve the exact residue: submitted envelopes, failed CAPA promotion, wrong-locus tasks 894/895, AGENTS.md edit-and-removal, and shell/Git roster rejection.
payload.preventive_action: Before any future CAPA buildout or authority-affecting mutation, require a preflight that proves: target locus, authorized principal, lifecycle mutation surface, CAPA disposition surface, repo mutation surface, and commit/push closeout path. If any closeout surface is blocked, do not mutate implementation or lifecycle state; submit/record a blocker instead.
payload.verification: A future repair must show the correct target locus, authorized closeout path, and disposition of wrong-locus residue before claiming completion.
